agent_notes: Quality-over-volume list for PAINTS: 32 included venues/routes, with no parks outside the v6 safety list.
Big operational caveat: Google rating/review floors, public-holiday handling, and 8-12 walk-in tolerance require host confirmation; many independent venues publish hours but not group walk-in policy.
Creative Walks limited to Holland Park and Kensington Gardens because both are PAINTS Premium Launch parks in the district.
Books & Coffee is the binding category: only a handful of borough venues truly combine independent books with coffee/wine/social seating.

venues.opening_hours: Tue 5:30pm-12am, Wed-Thu 5:30pm-12am, Fri-Sat 12pm-1am, Sun-Mon closed per venue site
venues.walk_in_policy: SevenRooms snippet states walk-ins welcome from 5:30pm; groups of 8+ must be confirmed
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Intimate wine/restaurant; group of 8-12 only at early slot or with host call
venues.capacity_notes: Small modern bistro; do not rely on peak walk-in
venues.why_fits_paints: Open-fire cooking, natural wine language and vinyl culture make it the borough’s strongest show-and-tell drinks pick. It feels local and textured rather than hotel-polished. The Golborne Road setting gives PAINTS a north-west counterweight.
venues.anecdote_story: Named for the Roman goddess of fire; press describes a wine bar/restaurant with open-fire cooking and music culture.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Produce-led plates, cocktails, £8 wines at Notti Hour, late-night music programming.
venues.safety_notes: Central/west London high-footfall streets; avoid late-night dispersal down quiet residential cut-throughs.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min walk - host must verify on Google Maps
venues.risks_watchouts: Opening hours, public-holiday service, Google rating/review floor, and 8-12 walk-in tolerance need host confirmation before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Visit Portobello says Mon-Sun 8am-7pm with traders usually 8:30am-6pm; Saturday is main day per Visit London
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; no individual walk-in booking required
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Open street market suits 8-12 if host uses meeting point and regroup stops
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor linear market; crowd management required on Saturdays
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the borough’s most story-rich market: antiques, food, fashion and layered street life. It is obvious, so it takes a penalty, but still clears because the format is ideal for show-and-tell objects. Host should design a quieter route north-to-south or south-to-north.
venues.anecdote_story: RBKC market documents trace Portobello/Golborne trading back to the 1860s with antiques becoming central in the 1950s.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Antiques, vintage, food stalls, street traders.
venues.safety_notes: Central/west London high-footfall streets; avoid late-night dispersal down quiet residential cut-throughs.
venues.risks_watchouts: Opening hours, public-holiday service, Google rating/review floor, and 8-12 walk-in tolerance need host confirmation before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Fri-Sun 9am-5pm; rain or shine per market site
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Works for 8-12 with set meeting point; avoid dense Saturday core if attendees are crowd-sensitive
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor/covered-under-Westway sections; variable stall attendance
venues.why_fits_paints: This is more PAINTS-coded than the postcard Portobello stretch because vintage, makers and collectors provide objects to discuss. The Westway setting gives weather resilience. It is good as either primary or backup to Portobello.
venues.anecdote_story: Market site positions it around vintage fashion, Sunday flea stalls, artisans and collectors.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Vintage fashion, antiques, makers, street food.
venues.safety_notes: Central/west London high-footfall streets; avoid late-night dispersal down quiet residential cut-throughs.

venues.opening_hours: Covered by RBKC/Portobello market sources; exact stall days vary - host must confirm
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access street market
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Works as a slower, less tourist-heavy market walk for 8-12
venues.capacity_notes: Street-market format; host should plan regroup points
venues.why_fits_paints: Golborne gives the brief a less polished north Kensington counterweight with food, flea-market texture and Trellick Tower context. It is strong culturally but operationally less neatly documented than farmers markets. Best paired with Caia or Portobello Green.
venues.anecdote_story: RBKC notes Golborne and Portobello developed as iconic markets from the 1860s; local plan references Golborne’s distinct market identity.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street-market goods, food, antiques/vintage depending on day.
venues.safety_notes: Central/west London high-footfall streets; avoid late-night dispersal down quiet residential cut-throughs.

venues.venue_name: Duke of York Square Food Market
venues.opening_hours: Every Saturday 10am-4pm per Real Food Festival/Chelsea Barracks sources
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 if meeting away from stall queues
venues.capacity_notes: Pedestrian square market; busy but manageable
venues.why_fits_paints: The market’s independent food-trader focus and Saatchi adjacency make it the strongest Chelsea market day. It is polished, but useful for a culture-plus-food route. Weather exposure and crowds keep it below the top Portobello score.
venues.anecdote_story: Real Food Festival says it continues a market legacy supporting independent artisans and producers.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Independent food traders, prepared food, produce.
venues.safety_notes: Central/west London high-footfall streets; avoid late-night dispersal down quiet residential cut-throughs.

venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access farmers market
venues.group_suitability_8_12: 8-12 easy if host meets outside school entrance and keeps tasting stops short
venues.capacity_notes: Schoolyard market; stall attendance varies weekly
venues.why_fits_paints: Producer names are visible and specific, which gives attendees things to compare and discuss. It is less performative than Portobello and better for food-led strangers. The short hours require punctual hosting.
venues.anecdote_story: London Farmers Markets lists named producers across dairy, bread, fish, preserves, flowers and prepared foods.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Farm produce, bread, cheese, fish, flowers, cooked foods and drinks.

venues.opening_hours: Every Saturday 9am-2pm, open weekly rain or shine per London Farmers Markets
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access farmers market
venues.group_suitability_8_12: 8-12 manageable if host starts on pedestrianised Bute Street
venues.capacity_notes: Small pedestrian street; go early for space
venues.why_fits_paints: Bute Street has a compact neighbourhood feel and producer-led food without needing a full tourist route. The “rain or shine” note helps reliability. It pairs naturally with South Kensington Books and museums.
venues.anecdote_story: London Farmers Markets describes Bute Street as a pedestrianised street near South Ken tube with fresh fish, sausage sandwiches and organic vegetables.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Farm produce, fish/shellfish, baked goods, cooked food.

venues.opening_hours: Every Sunday 10am-2pm; open every week rain or shine per London Farmers Markets
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access farmers market
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for Sunday 8-12 if meeting point is fixed
venues.capacity_notes: Tree-lined Phillimore Walk; variable stall attendance
venues.why_fits_paints: A calmer Sunday market option in the centre of the borough, useful when Saturday markets are too crowded. Producer focus is strong, but it has less story than Portobello. Works well before Holland Park or Japan House.
venues.anecdote_story: RBKC announced the market in 2020 in partnership with London Farmers Markets as a new outdoor shopping project.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fresh fish, seasonal fruit/veg, pies, bread, eggs, poultry, preserves and flowers.

venues.venue_name: Holland Park Kyoto Garden and Design Loop
venues.opening_hours: Park opens 7:30am until dusk; closing times displayed at entrances per RBKC
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park; no booking required
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for 8-12 in daylight with host-led stops
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor route; avoid dusk closure and peacock/crowd bottlenecks
venues.why_fits_paints: Holland Park is on the PAINTS Premium Launch safety list, and the Kyoto Garden gives the walk a clear visual anchor. The route can connect Japanese garden design, ecology, Leighton House and the Design Museum without leaving the approved safety framework. Best in daylight only.
venues.anecdote_story: RBKC says Kyoto Garden was donated by the Chamber of Commerce of Kyoto in 1991.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Stops: Kyoto Garden waterfall/pond, Holland House remains, ecology garden edge, Design Museum exterior/atrium.
venues.safety_notes: Creative Walk approved: Holland Park is on PAINTS Premium Launch list. Daylight only; park closes at dusk.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 5 min from High Street Kensington side - host must verify exact start
venues.risks_watchouts: Opening hours, public-holiday service, Google rating/review floor, and 8-12 walk-in tolerance need host confirmation before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Kensington Gardens opens 6am and closes at dusk; 2026 PDF lists 29 Jun-2 Aug close 21:30
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access Royal Park; no booking required
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for 8-12 in daylight, with wide paths and clear transit edges
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor route; use defined meeting point at Kensington Palace gates
venues.best_time_slot: Morning or late afternoon before dusk
venues.why_fits_paints: Kensington Gardens is a Premium Launch park and gives a safer, more legible royal-park walk than ad hoc street wandering. The Palace, Round Pond, Albert Memorial sightline and Serpentine edge provide easy story stops. It should not be run after dusk.
venues.anecdote_story: Royal Parks says the gardens were shaped from royal/private gardens associated with Queen Caroline and are now open to everyone.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Stops: Kensington Palace gates, Round Pond, Italian Gardens/sightline, Serpentine Pavilion or gallery edge.
venues.safety_notes: Creative Walk approved: Kensington Gardens is on PAINTS Premium Launch list. Stay on lit/high-footfall edges near closing time.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 5 min from Kensington High Street edge
venues.risks_watchouts: Opening hours, public-holiday service, Google rating/review floor, and 8-12 walk-in tolerance need host confirmation before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Mon-Thu 10am-5pm, Fri-Sun 10am-6pm per NIGO exhibition page
venues.walk_in_policy: Timed ticket for paid shows; members can turn up; host must confirm same-day availability
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 if host books/times entry; atrium supports gathering
venues.capacity_notes: Large museum; paid exhibitions may sell out
venues.why_fits_paints: NIGO: From Japan with Love is a current exhibition with objects, fashion, craft and cultural memory that attendees can debate. The building itself adds a design-history layer. Obvious for design lovers, but still PAINTS-perfect.
venues.anecdote_story: Current show is billed as the first major retrospective of NIGO, with hundreds of objects from his archive and craft/tea ceremony material noted by press.
venues.serve_or_on_view: NIGO: From Japan with Love; PLATFORM: Simone Brewster also running in 2026 per South Ken Culture Quarter.
venues.safety_notes: Central/west London high-footfall streets; avoid late-night dispersal down quiet residential cut-throughs.
venues.risks_watchouts: Opening hours, public-holiday service, Google rating/review floor, and 8-12 walk-in tolerance need host confirmation before publishing.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Kensington High Street / High Street Kensington
venues.opening_hours: Mon-Sat 10am-8pm, Sundays and public holidays 12pm-6pm per exhibition sources
venues.walk_in_policy: Free exhibition; ticket/entry availability should be checked by host
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 if timed away from peak retail traffic
venues.capacity_notes: Gallery format; host should check group policy
venues.why_fits_paints: The Kyotographie pairing gives a precise current exhibition rather than just a nice building. Photography creates accessible conversation hooks for strangers. It also pairs cleanly with Kensington Gardens or High Street Kensington market.
venues.anecdote_story: Japan House presents Kawada Kikuji and Iwane Ai in a rare UK presentation linked to KYOTOGRAPHIE.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Kyotographie: Kawada Kikuji x Iwane Ai, 3 Jun-18 Oct 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Central/west London high-footfall streets; avoid late-night dispersal down quiet residential cut-throughs.
venues.risks_watchouts: Opening hours, public-holiday service, Google rating/review floor, and 8-12 walk-in tolerance need host confirmation before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Wed-Mon 10am-5:30pm, last entry 4:30pm; closed Tuesdays per RBKC
venues.walk_in_policy: Admission ticket; host must confirm timed availability for 8-12
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 with timed museum entry and quiet route through house
venues.capacity_notes: Historic house; keep group together and avoid peak guided-tour congestion
venues.why_fits_paints: The Arab Hall exhibition is exactly the kind of place-based story PAINTS wants: architecture, collecting, ceramics and contemporary response in one room. It is less obvious than the big South Ken museums. Strong backup ecosystem nearby.
venues.anecdote_story: Former artist studio-home of Frederic Leighton; 2026 marks 100 years as a public museum and includes new research into the Arab Hall.
venues.serve_or_on_view: The Arab Hall: Past and Present, 21 Mar-4 Oct 2026, with installations/film and historic house access.
venues.safety_notes: Central/west London high-footfall streets; avoid late-night dispersal down quiet residential cut-throughs.
venues.risks_watchouts: Opening hours, public-holiday service, Google rating/review floor, and 8-12 walk-in tolerance need host confirmation before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: 10am-6pm Mar-Oct for gardens/exhibition areas per HRP/South Ken Culture Quarter; host must confirm palace ticket slots
venues.walk_in_policy: Timed ticket likely; host must confirm availability
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 if ticketed entry is secured; not true casual walk-in at peak
venues.capacity_notes: Major attraction; timed entry and tourist crowds
venues.why_fits_paints: It is obvious, but The Last Princesses of Punjab gives a timely, story-led exhibition with real conversation depth. The setting inside an approved park helps a hybrid walk/gallery day. Tier A only narrowly because ticketing must be managed.
venues.anecdote_story: Palace frames Sophia Duleep Singh as Queen Victoria’s goddaughter, Indian princess and suffragette campaigner.
venues.serve_or_on_view: The Last Princesses of Punjab plus state apartments/gardens.
venues.safety_notes: Central/west London high-footfall streets; avoid late-night dispersal down quiet residential cut-throughs.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 8 min walk from High Street Kensington side
venues.risks_watchouts: Opening hours, public-holiday service, Google rating/review floor, and 8-12 walk-in tolerance need host confirmation before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: 10am-5:50pm per South Ken Culture Quarter listing; host must confirm official NHM page before publishing
venues.walk_in_policy: Timed/free entry may require booking; host must confirm availability
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Works for 8-12 but crowd management essential
venues.capacity_notes: Large museum; busy with school/visitor groups
venues.why_fits_paints: The David Attenborough-linked Our Story exhibition gives a current hook, but the museum is highly obvious and crowded. It can work if framed around one exhibition rather than a general museum wander. Tier B due obviousness and crowd risk.
venues.anecdote_story: NHM’s collection scale and Attenborough collaboration make this a public-science story rather than just dinosaur tourism.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Our Story with David Attenborough, listed until 30 Aug 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Central/west London high-footfall streets; avoid late-night dispersal down quiet residential cut-throughs.

venues.opening_hours: 10am-6pm per South Ken Culture Quarter listing
venues.walk_in_policy: Free museum; timed/free entry availability should be checked
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 if focused on one exhibition
venues.capacity_notes: Large museum; crowd/school group risk
venues.why_fits_paints: Future of Food is practical, tactile and discussion-friendly, but less art/craft-heavy than PAINTS ideal. It is a solid operational backup in the museum quarter. Tier B because category fit is adjacent rather than perfect.
venues.anecdote_story: The exhibition examines sustainable ways to grow, produce, purchase, cook and eat food.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Future of Food, listed until 1 Sep 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Central/west London high-footfall streets; avoid late-night dispersal down quiet residential cut-throughs.
venues.risks_watchouts: Opening hours, public-holiday service, Google rating/review floor, and 8-12 walk-in tolerance need host confirmation before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Daily 10am-5:45pm, Friday until 10pm per exhibition listings
venues.walk_in_policy: Timed ticket for Schiaparelli; host must confirm availability
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for 8-12 with booked/timed exhibition slot
venues.capacity_notes: Large museum; ticketed exhibition can sell out
venues.best_time_slot: Friday early evening or weekday morning
venues.why_fits_paints: Schiaparelli: Fashion Becomes Art has the fashion-as-art argument and press attention needed for PAINTS. It gives immediate objects to discuss: surrealism, craft, collaboration and contemporary spectacle. Obviousness penalty applies, but the exhibition itself clears the bar.
venues.anecdote_story: Press highlights Schiaparelli’s surrealist collaborations with artists such as Dali and Cocteau and her fashion/art boundary-crossing.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Schiaparelli: Fashion Becomes Art, 28 Mar-8 Nov 2026.

venues.opening_hours: Tue-Fri 10:15am-5:45pm, Sat-Sun 11am-6pm per South Ken Culture Quarter listing
venues.walk_in_policy: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm walk-in/group policy
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 if museum confirms group entry
venues.capacity_notes: Smaller museum; group flow needs confirmation
venues.why_fits_paints: Shared Airs gives a distinctive music-culture angle inside the museum quarter, less obvious than the big three. It is not purely visual art, but folk traditions and instrument collections support show-and-tell conversation. Tier B because current press signal is lighter.
venues.anecdote_story: The Royal College of Music Museum connects performance history, instruments and contemporary folk traditions.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Shared Airs: Our Thriving Folk Musics, listed until 25 Oct 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Central/west London high-footfall streets; avoid late-night dispersal down quiet residential cut-throughs.

venues.opening_hours: 10am-5pm per South Ken Culture Quarter listing for Earth Photo
venues.walk_in_policy: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm walk-in/group entry
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Works for a focused 8-12 photo discussion if entry is confirmed
venues.capacity_notes: Institution/gallery setting; exact group capacity unverified
venues.why_fits_paints: Earth Photo gives image-led conversation around environment and place, and the RGS setting has intellectual character. It is short-run, so operational timing is the risk. Tier B due limited run and lighter amenities.
venues.anecdote_story: Earth Photo is framed around environmental issues and the impact of climate change.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Earth Photo Exhibition 2026, 26 Jun-24 Jul 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Central/west London high-footfall streets; avoid late-night dispersal down quiet residential cut-throughs.
venues.risks_watchouts: Opening hours, public-holiday service, Google rating/review floor, and 8-12 walk-in tolerance need host confirmation before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: The Sun and The Moon open daily from 10am, final entry 4:30pm per ticket page; lates on selected Fridays
venues.walk_in_policy: Ticketed exhibition; Saatchi Instagram says no pre-booking required, host must confirm same-day group entry
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 with ticket plan; large galleries help
venues.capacity_notes: Large gallery; lates are busier
venues.best_time_slot: Weekday 10:30am or Friday lates if ticketed
venues.why_fits_paints: The celestial exhibition is broad, visual and immediately discussable without specialist knowledge. The Duke of York Square pairing makes it easy to combine with a market day. It is polished but culturally robust.
venues.anecdote_story: The exhibition spans two floors/nine spaces and looks at sun/moon imagery across cultures and time.
venues.serve_or_on_view: The Sun and The Moon: Art Inspired by the Celestial, 5 Jun-8 Sep 2026; RHS Botanical Art also overlaps until 2 Aug.
venues.safety_notes: Central/west London high-footfall streets; avoid late-night dispersal down quiet residential cut-throughs.
venues.backup_venue: Duke of York Square Food Market
venues.backup_walk_time: 2 min walk; market operates Saturdays only

venues.capacity_notes: Commercial gallery; compact viewing likely
venues.why_fits_paints: Cadogan adds a quieter Chelsea contemporary gallery counterpoint to Saatchi. Its current programme includes painting/abstraction, but public operational details are thinner. Tier B because host confirmation is essential.
venues.anecdote_story: Cadogan describes itself as an independent contemporary art gallery with London and Milan spaces and a 40-year mission.

venues.opening_hours: Serpentine exhibition pages list 10am-6pm context; host must confirm exact gallery hours
venues.walk_in_policy: Free exhibition with walk-ups welcome for Hockney; queue possible
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 if host manages queue and park route
venues.capacity_notes: Free gallery/pavilion; queues likely for Hockney
venues.why_fits_paints: Hockney plus the 2026 Pavilion gives a high-quality art-and-architecture day inside an approved park setting. The official page explicitly notes walk-ups are welcome for the Hockney show, though queues may occur. Boundary caveat: Serpentine sits in Kensington Gardens but administrative boundary should be host-checked.
venues.anecdote_story: 2026 programme includes Hockney, Cecily Brown, Amar Kanwar and the 25th Pavilion; press describes LANZA atelier’s brick crinkle-crankle pavilion.
venues.serve_or_on_view: David Hockney: A Year in Normandie at Serpentine North to 23 Aug 2026; Serpentine Pavilion 2026 to Oct.

venues.opening_hours: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm current Chelsea shop/wine bar hours
venues.walk_in_policy: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm group walk-ins; events may occupy space
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best book pick for 8-12 if no event conflict and early slot
venues.capacity_notes: Bookshop/wine bar/events space; exact casual capacity unverified
venues.best_time_slot: Late morning coffee or early evening wine before events
venues.why_fits_paints: It directly satisfies the bookshop-plus-social-space rule, with literary events and a Chelsea location. The programme gives easy conversation starters without needing a separate café. Tier A despite operational caveat because category fit is unusually strong.
venues.anecdote_story: BookBar says it brings people together through books; Chelsea events include named author conversations.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Books, coffee/wine bar/social space, author events and BookClub.

venues.venue_name: South Kensington Books + Bute Street coffee pairing
venues.opening_hours: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm bookshop and coffee partner hours
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop walk-in likely; group coffee seating next door/nearby must be confirmed
venues.group_suitability_8_12: 8-12 can browse bookshop then split/settle at nearby independent cafe if seats allow
venues.capacity_notes: Bookshop browsing fine; two-hour seating depends on cafe partner
venues.why_fits_paints: The art/travel book range and museum-quarter location make this a better PAINTS bookshop than a generic high-street branch. It needs a literal coffee pairing rather than claiming an onsite café. Tier B because seating is the weak link.
venues.anecdote_story: Independent bookseller in the museum quarter with visible art-books category and staff picks online.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Independent bookshop; pair with independent Bute Street/South Ken coffee after host confirmation.

venues.venue_name: The Notting Hill Bookshop + Bookshop Café
venues.opening_hours: Cafe source lists 8am-6pm Saturday; Instagram/travel sources say everyday 9am-7pm for bookshop/cafe - host must confirm
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-in likely for browsing/cafe; group seating for 8-12 is unverified and probably tight
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best as browse plus short coffee; two-hour seated format needs host confirmation
venues.capacity_notes: Small cafe; avoid peak weekend Portobello crowds
venues.why_fits_paints: This is one of the few borough options where the bookshop and café are physically connected in the same micro-place. It is obvious due film associations, but the current café makes it operationally relevant. Better for a gentle morning than a long table event.
venues.anecdote_story: Blenheim Crescent is associated with the Travel Bookshop inspiration behind Notting Hill; current shop occupies the cultural memory of that site.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Bookshop plus adjacent/companion café with coffee and pastries.
venues.safety_notes: Central/west London high-footfall streets; avoid late-night dispersal down quiet residential cut-throughs.

venues.capacity_notes: Independent bookshop; no verified onsite cafe
venues.why_fits_paints: Literary-agent founders and a curated independent selection give it much more story than a chain. It only qualifies through a nearby coffee pairing, so it is Tier B. Use as a quieter alternative to the film-famous shop.
venues.anecdote_story: Founded in 2009 by literary agents Sarah Lutyens and Felicity Rubinstein; press/shop guides note curated fiction, poetry, art and children’s sections.

venues.venue_name: Partridges of Kensington + South Kensington Books pairing
venues.opening_hours: Partridges Instagram/site says Gloucester Road grocer open daily 7am-10pm; South Kensington Books hours unverified
venues.walk_in_policy: Grocer/cafe/goods walk-in likely; bookshop leg separate and must be confirmed
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Only works as a hybrid browse-and-coffee route, not a single seated bookshop cafe
venues.capacity_notes: Two-site format; host must manage movement and seating
venues.why_fits_paints: This is a stretch pick but gives the borough a food-literary route anchored by a family-run royal-warrant grocer and independent bookshop. It stays barely above floor because the book and coffee components are not literally in one venue. Use only if host wants South Ken and confirms seating.
venues.anecdote_story: Partridges says it has served Kensington & Chelsea since 1972 and now operates from Gloucester Road after leaving Duke of York Square.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Speciality grocery/coffee-food stop plus independent bookshop browse nearby.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Cemetery walks are not permitted by PAINTS v6 except official Highgate Cemetery tours; Brompton is explicitly listed as do not source.
Hyde Park is safety-shortlisted, but it is not meaningfully Kensington and Chelsea for this district and Kensington Gardens is the stronger approved option.
Inside a department-store retail environment and not a substantive bookshop/coffee concept; fails PAINTS anti-mall/templated filter.
Chain bookshop; PAINTS v6 says no chains and raised Books & Coffee bar needs independent literary identity.
Too obvious/TikTok-tourist-coded for PAINTS Tier A, and group walk-in reliability is poor at peak.
Useful backup but not included as primary because Cubitt House group polish and reservation posture make it less PAINTS-coded than smaller pubs.
Strong cultural fit, but current source showed next opening on 7 July 2026, after the 4 July run date; not treated as current for this output.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Partridges old Duke of York Square shop
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: The Chelsea shop closed in February 2025; market continues under separate arrangements, so the shop itself cannot be sourced.
